title: Rookie's Near No-Hitter Thrills Fenway
story: The crowd at Fenway Park held its breath. Red Sox rookie Bob Ojeda was just three outs away from baseball history on September 12, 1981. For eight nail-biting innings, he hadn't allowed a single hit against the mighty New York Yankees. But baseball can change in a heartbeat. As the ninth inning began, the Yankees' Rick Cerone and Dave Winfield spoiled Ojeda's dream with back-to-back doubles. Still, it was an unforgettable day for the young pitcher.
activity_question: How many innings did Bob Ojeda pitch without allowing a hit?
follow_up_question: What is a no-hitter in baseball?

title: Curious Dog Leads to Ancient Art Discovery
story: A playful pup sparked an incredible discovery in 1940. Four teenage boys were exploring near Lascaux, France, when their dog disappeared down a hole. Following their furry friend, the boys stumbled upon something extraordinary: a hidden cave filled with colorful paintings. title: The Wildest Soccer Score Ever
story: Imagine a soccer game where one team scores so many goals, they run out of room on the scoreboard! title: Luna 2: First Spacecraft to Hit the Moon
story: Zoom! A metal ball streaked through space, racing towards the moon. This was Luna 2, a Soviet spacecraft on a historic mission. It proved we could send objects beyond Earth's orbit, paving the way for future lunar missions.

story: Gunshots echoed through Italian theaters as A Fistful of Dollars premiered on September 12, 1964. title: Stargazer's Surprise Sparks Cosmic Catalog

bonus_fact: Natural rubber comes from trees, but synthetic rubber can be made in a laboratory.

title: Trailblazing Alice Joins the Police Force
story: Alice Stebbins Wells made history in 1910 when she pinned on her badge. The Los Angeles Police Department hired her as the first known female police officer in the United States. title: The Man Behind Q's Gadgets
story: Desmond Llewelyn, the actor who brought Q to life in James Bond films, was born in Newport, Wales in 1914. On September 12 of that year, the future gadget master entered the world. Llewelyn's journey took him from World War II veteran and prisoner of war to becoming the beloved quartermaster in 17 Bond movies over 36 years.
activity_question: What role did Desmond Llewelyn play in James Bond films?
bonus_fact: Q stands for 'Quartermaster', the person who supplies equipment to secret agents.
